summary:
Embrasures caused by divergences primarily occur on the lingual-faciotongue margin; they result when there’s an opening or gap formed where two adjacent tooth crowns meet but have not been brought into perfect fit through restorative procedures. These openings typically manifest linguofacially because of how dentition normally converges slightly toward each other upon eruption and subsequent wear patterns favor maintaining proper bite mechanics without excessive interproximal contacts. Sentence count: 4/5.
To summarize concisely focusing only on essential information related directly back to your query about 'divergence' affecting embrasures specifically within context provided earlier regarding its location relative to facial vs tongue aspects:

Embrasures stemming from divergence mostly develop on the lingual-facio-tongue aspect given typical anatomical tendencies promoting minor bucco-lingual inclinations post-tooth development and usage which ensures optimal functional integrity despite small separations forming between neighboring teeth cusps. Therefore confirming A as accurate based purely off core principles discussed herein concerning spatial relationships among maxillofacial elements under varying degrees of separation induced intentionally via orthodontic interventions etc...
